seed_flags.rationale: Touches the UoA-sensitive topic of Liability Limitations and Exclusions; exact alignment is not obvious from the clause text. Contract Manager should confirm against: Preferred: Total liability (including under indemnities offered) limited to the lesser of: (a) amount paid under the contract / contract value (subject to liability limit in Research Contract Delegations) OR (b) NZD$500,000. Limited to direct losses caused by negligent acts/omissions or wilful misconduct of the University. Consequential or indirect losses, 'hold harmless', and loss of data/profit/revenue/savings/business/goodwill explicitly excluded. | Acceptable: Total liability limit (including indemnities) in line with Research Contract Delegations. Uncapped liability acceptable ONLY for Trusted Funders on contestable rounds. Indirect losses acceptable only if total exposure is capped per Delegations. Carve-outs from cap acceptable for: Fraud, Wilful default, Personal injury/death, statutory non-excludable liability, and confidentiality (only if client revenue < NZD$150M). | Escalation: Refer Research Grants and Contracts Delegations (and Portfolio Manager for Trusted Funders on non-contestable rounds).

augmented_flags.clause_title: The term “Material” means material, other than human gametes or embryos, which

As such, it falls outside the scope of existing UoA policies and templates.

augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the transfer of data, which is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. No specific policy or template reference applies.

augmented_flags.clause_title: The Recipient Institution will hold the Material and Data on the terms of this
augmented_flags.snippet: The Recipient Institution will hold the Material and Data on the terms of this Agreement and solely for the purpose of the Study as described in Appendix 1, within the research group of [REDACTED] (the “Recipient Scientist”).
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the handling and purpose of Material and Data within the Recipient Institution's research group, which is not explicitly covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of the defined policy and template references.

augmented_flags.clause_title: The Recipient Institution hereby agrees to comply and procure that the Recipient
augmented_flags.snippet: The Recipient Institution hereby agrees to comply and procure that the Recipient Scientist and all personnel who work with the Material and Data comply with the following terms and conditions:  The Human Tissue Act 2004 applies to the “authorised activities” principally the removal, storage and use of “Relevant Materials” (as defined under the Act, including human cells, tissue and organs, but not cell lines) which come from a living or deceased person for “Scheduled Purposes” (these are set out in Schedule 1 of the Act, including, but not limited to, “research in connection with disorders, or the function of the human body”; “education or training relating to human health”; and “transplantation”).
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses compliance with the Human Tissue Act 2004, which governs the handling of human tissue for specific purposes. This topic is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy tiers and is flagged as blue.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 1. The Recipient Institution will not use the Material for administration to hum
augmented_flags.snippet: 1. The Recipient Institution will not use the Material for administration to human subjects or human application as that term is defined in the Human Tissue (Quality and Safety for Human Application) Regulations 2007 (or equivalent as each may be replaced or amended from time to time) or for clinical or diagnostic purposes.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses restrictions on the use of materials for human administration, clinical, or diagnostic purposes. This topic is not covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of existing guidance and is flagged as blue.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 2. The Recipient Institution may use the Material and Data solely for the purpos
augmented_flags.snippet: 2. The Recipient Institution may use the Material and Data solely for the purposes of the Study and as described in Appendix 1, from the date of receipt of the M2 aterial. The Recipient Institution will comply fully with all applicable environmental, health and safety laws, the Human Tissue Act 2004 and all Applicable Laws , rules, regulations, codes of conduct, codes of practice, research governance or ethical guidelines, and/or guidance, directions, decisions, determinations, or other requirements issued, from time to time, by any regulatory authority, that may apply with respect to its handling (including, but not limited to, storage, transport, use, processing, disposal or return) of the Material and all applicable laws, or other requirements issued, from time to time, by any regulatory authority, that may apply to the use, processing, storage, transfer and/or disposal of the Data.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the use of materials and data, compliance with laws, and ethical guidelines, which are not explicitly covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of the defined positions and templates, resulting in a blue flag.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 3. The Parties shall use a courier with suitable skill and experience to safely
augmented_flags.snippet: 3. The Parties shall use a courier with suitable skill and experience to safely transport the Material in accordance with all Applicable Laws. The provider Institution will bear the cost of carriage and any necessary insurance. The Provider Institution makes no charge for the Material. Risk in and responsibility for the Material shall pass to the Recipient Institution once it is received at its premises. If so requested by the Provider Institution the Recipient Institution shall provide it with written confirmation of the safe receipt of the Materials promptly after their delivery to the Recipient Institution’s laboratory.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the logistics of transporting materials, including courier requirements, cost allocation, and risk transfer. This topic is not explicitly covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of the existing policy framework and is flagged as blue.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 4. The Recipient Institution understands that the Material may have hazardous
augmented_flags.snippet: 4. The Recipient Institution understands that the Material may have hazardous properties, contain infectious agents or pose other health and safety risks. Subject to clause 12, the Provider Institution makes no represen…
augmented_flags.rationale: This clause addresses liability limitations and exclusions, which are sensitive under UoA Position #POS-07. While the clause excludes liability for the Provider Institution and imposes obligations on the Recipient Institution to use the Material responsibly, it does not explicitly align with the Preferred or Acceptable tier for liability limitations. Specifically, the clause does not mention a monetary cap on liability or carve-outs for statutory non-excludable liabilities, fraud, or personal injury/death. The absence of these elements requires further review to ensure compliance with UoA's liability standards. Escalation to the Research Grants and Contracts Delegations is recommended for confirmation.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 5. Recipient shall take appropriate measures to ensure a level of security
augmented_flags.snippet: 5. Recipient shall take appropriate measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including amongst other things, as appropriate: i. the encryption of the Data; ii. the ability to ensure the on-going confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of systems and services for managing and storing the Data; iii. the ability to restore the availability and access to the Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident; and iv. having and implementing a process for regularly testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the management and storage of the Data.
augmented_flags.rationale: This clause addresses data security measures, which indirectly relate to confidentiality obligations under UoA Position #POS-11. While the clause does not explicitly conflict with UoA's preferred or acceptable positions on confidentiality, it introduces additional obligations (e.g., encryption, resilience, restoration, and testing) that are not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or Standard Template. These measures are reasonable but require confirmation that they align with UoA's operational capabilities and compliance framework. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is recommended to ensure alignment with UoA's confidentiality and data security standards.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 6. The Recipient shall keep all of the Data secure from any unauthorised or
augmented_flags.snippet: 6. The Recipient shall keep all of the Data secure from any unauthorised or accidental use, access, disclosure, damage, loss or destruction. The Recipient will not permit the Data or any part of it to come into the possession or control of any individual other than those of its employees or students involved in the Research who are under the direct supervision of the Recipient Scientist. The Recipient shall comply with any request from the Provider to amend, rectify, transfer, block or destroy any of the Data.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses data security and management obligations, which are not explicitly covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of the existing policy framework and is flagged as blue.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 7. Except to the extent prohibited by law and subject to clause 12, the Recipien
augmented_flags.snippet: 7. Except to the extent prohibited by law and subject to clause 12, the Recipient Institution assumes all liability for damages which may arise from its receipt, use, storage or disposal of the Material and/or Data. The maximum aggregate liability of the Recipient Institution to the Provider in relation to this Agreement, including any under indemnity, is limited to an amount equal to NZD 100,000 but only to the extent that such liability can be limited in law and such limitation shall not apply to any liability arising from the Recipient Institution’s wilful misconduct or gross negligence, or any misuse of the Material or Data.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause limits the Recipient Institution's liability to NZD 100,000, which is well below the NZD 500,000 cap specified in UoA's Preferred Position (POS-07). While the clause includes carve-outs for wilful misconduct, gross negligence, and misuse of materials, these are consistent with UoA's Acceptable tier. The 'assumes all liability' phrasing is mitigated by the low monetary cap, making the clause acceptable under UoA's guidelines. Escalation is not required as the liability exposure is within acceptable limits.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 8. The Recipient Institution shall indemnify the Providing Institution, the
augmented_flags.snippet: 8. The Recipient Institution shall indemnify the Providing Institution, the Providing Institution’s employees in full against all liability, loss, damages or expenses (including legal and other professional fees and expenses) from or in connection with the handling, storage or disposal of the Material and/or Data by or on behalf of the University.
augmented_flags.rationale: This clause conflicts with UoA Position #POS-07 on Liability Limitations and Exclusions. The clause imposes an uncapped indemnity obligation ('in full against all liability') on the Recipient Institution, which is not aligned with UoA's preferred or acceptable positions. UoA's preferred position limits total liability (including under indemnities) to the lesser of the contract value or NZD$500,000, and excludes consequential or indirect losses. Acceptable deviations require liability caps in line with Research Contract Delegations or specific carve-outs for limited scenarios. This clause does not meet these criteria and must be escalated to the Research Grants and Contracts Delegations for review.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 9. The liability of either party for any breach of this Agreement, or arising in
augmented_flags.snippet: 9. The liability of either party for any breach of this Agreement, or arising in any other way out of the subject matter of this Agreement, will not extend to loss of business or profit, or to any indirect or consequential damages or losses.
augmented_flags.rationale: This clause explicitly excludes liability for indirect or consequential losses, as well as loss of business or profit. This aligns with the UoA Preferred Position under POS-07, which requires such exclusions. While the clause does not explicitly address the total liability cap, the exclusion of indirect and consequential losses is sufficient to meet the Preferred tier for this specific aspect of liability limitations.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 10. The Recipient Institution agrees to obtain the written consent of the Provid
augmented_flags.snippet: 10. The Recipient Institution agrees to obtain the written consent of the Provider Institution if there is any material change to the proposed use of the Material in the Study as described in Appendix 1.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses a topic (material change to the use of materials) that is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. No specific policy or template reference applies.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 11. The Parties will aim to co-author any publications arising from this Agreeme
augmented_flags.snippet: 11. The Parties will aim to co-author any publications arising from this Agreement. For publications arising from use of the Material and/or Data not authored by the Provider Institution, the Recipient Scientist will ac…
augmented_flags.rationale: This clause addresses the topic of publication and confidentiality. While the clause preserves the right to publish (co-authoring, acknowledgment, and providing a copy), it introduces a requirement for written permission from the Provider Institution for publishing confidential information. This aligns with UoA's Preferred Position for publication rights (POS-12) but introduces additional confidentiality obligations that require review. The confidentiality aspect must be checked against UoA Position #POS-11 to ensure compliance with UoA's preferred or acceptable confidentiality terms. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts, is recommended for confirmation.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 12. The Provider Institution undertakes that where required by Applicable Laws t
augmented_flags.snippet: 12. The Provider Institution undertakes that where required by Applicable Laws the Material has been obtained from humans with the appropriate consent as required by the Human Tissue Act 2004 and with ethical approval. The Provider Institution retains ownership or custodian rights in the Materials and Data (and any unmodified derivatives of the Materials) it provides as part of this Agreement and hereby grants to the Recipient Institution a non-exclusive, royalty-free licence to use the Material and Data for the Study only.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses compliance with the Human Tissue Act 2004 and ethical approval for human-derived materials, as well as ownership and licensing terms. This topic is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy tiers and is flagged as blue.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 13. The Recipient Institution shall keep all information relating to the Materia
augmented_flags.snippet: 13. The Recipient Institution shall keep all information relating to the Material and Data confidential and shall not use it for any purpose other than as provided in this Agreement. This shall not apply to information …
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses confidentiality but does not explicitly align with UoA's Preferred Position under POS-11. While it excludes public, independently developed, and legally required disclosures, it does not mention UoA's ability to disclose to agents and Affiliates or specify a duration for confidentiality obligations (e.g., up to 5 years post-completion). This deviation places the clause in the Acceptable tier, but it requires confirmation that the confidentiality duration is limited (e.g., 5–7 years). Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts, is required if these elements are not clarified.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 14. Any results generated in the course of the Study from the Recipient’s use of
augmented_flags.snippet: 14. Any results generated in the course of the Study from the Recipient’s use of the Materials and/or Data shall be jointly owned by the Parties. Each Party may use the results for its own teaching and non-commercial research purposes, provided that such use is subject to the prior written consent of the other Party. Each Party retains ownership of all rights (including Intellectual Property Rights) in its information, materials and other items existing prior to or developed independently of this Agreement.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses Intellectual Property (IP) ownership and use of results, which falls under the topic of 'Intellectual Property' as defined in UoA Position #POS-IP. UoA's Preferred Contracting Positions do not provide specific guidance on IP, and all IP clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices (Head of IP) for review. This clause is flagged as AMBER to ensure it is reviewed by the appropriate authority.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 15. The Recipient undertakes not to attempt to identify or contact any individua
augmented_flags.snippet: 15. The Recipient undertakes not to attempt to identify or contact any individual to whom the Data relates (Data Subject). In the event that the Recipient inadvertently identifies any Data Subject, then it will notify
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses data protection and privacy obligations, specifically regarding the identification and handling of Data Subjects under the UK GDPR and Data Protection Act (2018). This topic is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy tiers and is flagged as blue.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 16. Nothing included in this Agreement shall prevent the Provider Institution fr
augmented_flags.snippet: 16. Nothing included in this Agreement shall prevent the Provider Institution from being able to distribute the Material and/or Data to other entities as described in Appendix 1. If, as per the details included in Appendix 1, the Material and/or Data is to be transferred to another institution for the purposes of the Study, the responsibility for compliance with the terms of this Agreement rests with the Recipient Institution.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the Provider Institution's ability to distribute Material and/or Data to other entities and assigns compliance responsibility to the Recipient Institution. This topic is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy tiers and is flagged as blue.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 17. The Provider Institution has the right to terminate this agreement forthwith
augmented_flags.snippet: 17. The Provider Institution has the right to terminate this agreement forthwith at any time by means of written notice to Recipient Institution if the ethical approval is withdrawn or if the Recipient Institution is in breach of this Agreement.
augmented_flags.rationale: This clause addresses termination rights, which is a UoA-sensitive topic. While it allows termination for breach or withdrawal of ethical approval, it does not provide mutual two-way termination rights or explicitly address accrued rights and benefits, termination costs, or uncancellable obligations as required by UoA's Preferred Position. However, termination for breach and ethical approval withdrawal is not inherently outside the Acceptable tier, provided it aligns with the specific context of the agreement. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts, is recommended to confirm alignment with UoA's policy and ensure no material risks are introduced.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 18. This Agreement shall not be amended except in writing by duly authorised
augmented_flags.snippet: 18. This Agreement shall not be amended except in writing by duly authorised signatories of the Parties hereto.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause aligns with the UoA Preferred Contracting Position POS-21 on 'Variations and Extensions,' which requires all variations to be negotiated and executed in writing. This clause matches the preferred tier as it ensures amendments are formalised in writing by authorised signatories.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 19. The Parties agree that this Agreement and any subsequent amendments to it
augmented_flags.snippet: 19. The Parties agree that this Agreement and any subsequent amendments to it shall be binding on their successors in title, and assigns, and undertake to take all necessary steps to ensure that they are so bound.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the binding nature of the Agreement on successors and assigns, which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of the defined policy and template references.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 20. This Agreement does not create any right enforceable by any person not a Par
augmented_flags.snippet: 20. This Agreement does not create any right enforceable by any person not a Party to it.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses third-party enforceability, which is not covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of existing guidance.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 21. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties in
augmented_flags.snippet: 21. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties in relation to its subject matter and no statements or representations made by any Party have been relied upon by the other in entering into this Agreement.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause matches the standard template wording for Material Transfer Agreements, specifically the 'Entire Agreement' provision. This ensures clarity and prevents reliance on external representations, aligning with UoA's standard practice.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 22. The rights and obligations of the Parties are personal and may not be assign
augmented_flags.snippet: 22. The rights and obligations of the Parties are personal and may not be assigned at any time without the prior written consent of the other Party which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld; provided that it shall be a requirement in all cases of assignation that the assignee undertakes to perform all outstanding obligations of the assignor as though the assignee had been an original party hereto.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses assignment of rights and obligations, which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for Material Transfer Agreements. As such, it falls outside the scope of pre-defined policy and template references.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 23. Neither party shall use the names or trademarks of the other party or of any
augmented_flags.snippet: 23. Neither party shall use the names or trademarks of the other party or of any of the other party's affiliated entities in any advertising, publicity, endorsement, or promotion unless the other party has provided prior written consent for the particular use contemplated.
augmented_flags.rationale: This clause aligns with UoA Position #POS-14 on Publicity, which states that neither party can use names or brands of the other or their personnel without prior written consent. The clause explicitly requires prior written consent for any use of names or trademarks, which matches the Preferred tier of the UoA Position.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 24. This Agreement shall be governed by English Law, and the English Courts shal
augmented_flags.snippet: 24. This Agreement shall be governed by English Law, and the English Courts shall have exclusive jurisdiction to deal with any dispute which may arise out of or in connection with this Letter Agreement.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause specifies English Law as the governing law and grants exclusive jurisdiction to the English Courts. This aligns with the Acceptable tier under UoA Position #POS-16, which permits the United Kingdom as an acceptable jurisdiction. However, the exclusive jurisdiction aspect requires confirmation to ensure it does not conflict with UoA's preference for non-exclusive jurisdiction in certain cases. Escalation to General Counsel is recommended for final review.

augmented_flags.clause_title: 25. This Agreement shall be effective as of the date of the last signature below
augmented_flags.snippet: 25. This Agreement shall be effective as of the date of the last signature below and shall terminate upon termination of the Study in Appendix 1 or 2 years from the effective date, whichever is sooner.
augmented_flags.rationale: This clause addresses the term and termination of the agreement, which is a UoA-sensitive topic. While it specifies a fixed term (2 years or upon termination of the Study), it does not explicitly address mutual two-way termination for material breach, insolvency, or UoA's right to terminate for non-payment, as required by the Preferred Position. However, it does not introduce termination for convenience, which would be unacceptable outside Consulting contracts. The clause falls within the Acceptable tier for agreements where termination provisions cannot always be altered, such as NZ and International Funding Agency contracts. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts, is recommended for confirmation.
